target.name: Atana Elements
target.founder: Tom Wilson (CEO, Stanford GSB + Royal School of Mines, ex-Hess Corp, was early hire and operations/resource lead at Lilac Solutions from 2019)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $28M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Atana Elements is a seed-stage energy and climate technology company founded by Tom Wilson, whose background includes operations leadership at Lilac Solutions and prior roles at Hess Corporation. The company has raised $27.5M to date. The comparable universe consists of three private peers in the energy and climate sector—Hyperscale Power, Voya Energy, and Maritime Fusion—with no public market comps available.

Stage-progression analysis, the applicable triangulation method given data constraints, applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement to the $27.5M raised, yielding a median implied valuation of $69M. This method assumes capital deployed reflects substantive progress toward Series A or equivalent inflection. The low and high bounds of the range are $58M and $79M, respectively, derived from sensitivity around the step-up multiplier.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $58M to $79M, with a median of $69M. Material uncertainties include the absence of public comps and reliance on a single quantitative method; deeper insight would require revenue traction, customer commitments, or comparable transaction data from the three identified private peers. Founder experience and operational track record provide context but do not independently move valuation in this framework.
